summary: This satirical piece parodies the popular 'Falsehoods Programmers Believe' format by applying it to dogs instead of technical topics. Rather than exposing edge cases in date handling or name validation, Wayne constructs a playful logical argument that all dogs are good dogs, all dogs are the best dogs, and nothing is better than dogs. The piece subverts reader expectations by using a technical writing format for pure wholesome content about canine appreciation.
key_points: Parodies the 'Falsehoods Programmers Believe' blog post genre that typically addresses technical edge cases
Constructs a mock-logical argument that leads to the conclusion all dogs are equally the best
Includes practical wisdom disguised as jokes (don't get a dog if you can't guarantee responsibility)
Uses the numbered list format to create escalating absurdity culminating in 'Dogs.'
money_quotes: Okay, all dogs are good dogs, but there's only one best dog.
There are times when you shouldn't be petting a dog.
What about if you're a super spy and you have to choose between booping a nose and saving Wichita from an orbital laser cannon?
I should get a dog if I can't guarantee 100% responsibility.
Programming is better than playing with dogs.
There is something better than dogs.
key_insight: Sometimes the best use of a technical writing format is to remind us that all dogs are good dogs.
